data.title: U.S. Futures, European Stocks Rise as Upbeat Corporate Earnings Boost Optimism
data.description:
U.S. futures for the S&P 500 were up 0.1% and futures for the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.2%. Changes in futures do not necessarily predict movements after the opening bell.

In Europe, the Stoxx Europe 600 climbed 0.3% in morning trading. Vestas Wind Systems added 11.6% and Saab Series B rose 11.1%. On the other hand, GSK lost 6.4%, and Stellantis fell 4.2%. The FTSE 100 added 0.1%. Other stocks in Europe were up as France's CAC 40 increased 0.4% and Germany's DAX rose 0.3%.

The Wall Street Journal Dollar Index fell 0.1% to 95.57.

In commodities, Brent crude gained 0.5% to $69.84 a barrel, and WTI crude gained 0.5% to $67.90 a barrel. The European benchmark price for natural gas, the Dutch futures contract TTF, was up 0.6% to 35.02 euros a megawatt hour.

The German 10-year Bund yield climbed by 1 basis point to 2.694% from 2.68%, and the yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury fell by 1 basis point to 4.442% from 4.457%. Bond prices and yields move in opposite directions.

Stocks in Asia were mixed as Japan's Nikkei 225 index declined 0.2%, whereas Hong Kong's Hang Seng gained 1.2%. China's benchmark Shanghai Composite climbed 0.5%.

-This article was automatically generated by MarketWatch.

Write to Barcelona Editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: KBR wins FEED contract for KEPPT’s fertilizer facility in Iraq
data.description:
KBR has been awarded a front-end engineering design (FEED) contract for the development of an ammonia and urea production plant by KAR Electrical Power Production Trading FZE (KEPPT) in Basra, Iraq, as per Hydrocarbonprocessing.Under the terms of the contract, KBR will provide FEED for the 2,300 metric tons per day (tpd) ammonia production facility and a 3,850-tpd urea production unit. The FEED will be executed utilizing KBR’s proprietary ammonia technology, designed to enable high efficiency, low emissions, and operational reliability to assist KEPPT with its goal to achieve the lowest overall capex with an optimized project schedule.“We are honored to support this pivotal project, which monetizes gas feedstock to boost the agricultural industry in Iraq,” said Jay Ibrahim, President, KBR Sustainable Technology Solutions. “Underlined by KBR’s expertise in market-leading ammonia solutions and proven FEED capability, this initiative should generate employment and reduce the dependency of fertilizer imports, while repositioning Iraq as a global ammonia producer.”

mrchub.com

data.title: RCS - Carr's Group PLC - Carr’s Group enters partnership with Vétalis
data.description:
RNS Number : 6590R Carr's Group PLC 18 July 2025  

18 July 2025

Carr's Group plc

("Carr's Group", the "Group" or the "Company")

Carr's Group enters strategic manufacturing partnership with Vétalis

Carr's Group plc , a global leader in sustainable livestock supplements, announces it has entered into a strategic manufacturing partnership with French company Vétalis to develop an advanced range of Tracesure® boluses, with distribution scheduled from Autumn 2025.

The new partnership follows the strategic decision to close the ANIMAX production facility in June 2025 and enables the Group to allocate more resources toward innovation and meeting the demands of a growing market. Vétalis is a recognised expert in precision bolus supplementation technology and brings strong capabilities in developing science-backed solutions that support livestock health and sustainable farming practices.

The partnership marks a pivotal step in the Group's growth strategy by improving on-farm productivity which in turn supports sustainable global food security. By combining the Group's deep understanding of livestock nutrition with Vétalis' advanced manufacturing, the partnership will propel innovation, drive operational efficiencies, and improve product delivery. The agreement is expected to deliver meaningful operational savings while reinforcing the market-leading position of Tracesure® in the UK and Ireland. 

Joshua Hoopes, Chief Executive Officer of Carr's Group plc commented: "This is a key strategic milestone as we reposition the Group as a focused, pure-play agriculture business. Partnering with Vétalis enables us to build a more efficient platform for sustainable growth, while strengthening our customer offering and creating long-term value for shareholders."

For further information, please contact:

Enquiries

Carr's Group plc

+44 (0) 1228 554 600

Joshua Hoopes, Chief Executive Officer

Gavin Manson, Chief Financial Officer

Hudson Sandler

+44 (0) 20 7796 4133

Hattie Dreyfus / Nick Moore

About Carr's Group plc:

Carr's Group plc is an international, pure-play specialist agriculture manufacturer and provider of research-proven, value-added livestock supplements.

Carr's Group's mission is to drive sustainable global food security through enhancements to pasture grazing productivity, enabled by research-based products that optimise livestock performance and profitability for farmers. The Group produces nutritional animal supplements including feed licks, blocks, bagged minerals, and boluses for cattle, sheep, goats and horses. Its globally recognised brands include SmartLic®, Crystalyx® and Horslyx®.

Headquartered in Carlisle, Cumbria, Carr's Group operates 7 manufacturing sites across the UK, Germany and the USA, and serves customers in more than 20 countries through its expansive global distribution and support network.

Carr's Group plc has been listed on the London Stock Exchange since 1972 under the ticker "CARR".

For more information, visit: https://www.carrsgroup.com/

This information is provided by Reach, the non-regulatory press release distribution service of RNS, part of the London Stock Exchange. data.title: RentGuarantor to Commence Trading on London's AIM in mid-August
data.description:
RentGuarantor (RGG.AQ) shares will start trading on the AIM segment of the London stock exchange from 8 am local time on Aug. 15.

The rent guarantee services company's shares will be delisted from the Aquis Stock Exchange Growth Market's Apex Segment, according to a Friday release.

data.title: Solarius Capital Acquisition Closes IPO
data.description:
Solarius Capital Acquisition (SOCA, , SOCAW) said Thursday it has closed its initial public offering of 17.25 million units, including 2.25 million units sold to underwriters, at a price of $10 per unit.

Each unit consists of one share and half a redeemable warrant to purchase a share for $11.50, the blank-check company said.

The units are trading on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol, "SOCAU." The shares and warrants will trade under the symbols, "SOCA" and "SOCAW," respectively, once they begin trading separately.

data.title: Valterra Platinum Set for Stronger Third Quarter on Rising PGM Prices — Market Talk
data.description: Valterra Platinum's third-quarter earnings should improve substantially as production recovers and it gets a boost from rising platinum group metals prices, RBC Capital Markets analysts write. This follows a warning from the miner Friday that it expects a fall in first-half headline earnings due to lower sales volumes and costs associated with its demerger from Anglo American. Average PGM prices are up 24% since the beginning of June and its production is recovering, the analysts write. Its London-listed shares rise 4.3% to 3,630 pence.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

data.title: UK Market Talk Roundup: Shares Gaining
data.description:
Stocks on the rise in the UK today. Compiled by Dow Jones Newswires Markets Desk, markets.eu@dowjones.com

0748 GMT - Valterra Platinum's third-quarter earnings should improve substantially as production recovers and it gets a boost from rising platinum group metals prices, RBC Capital Markets analysts write. This follows a warning from the miner Friday that it expects a fall in first-half headline earnings due to lower sales volumes and costs associated with its demerger from Anglo American. Average PGM prices are up 24% since the beginning of June and its production is recovering, the analysts write. Its London-listed shares rise 4.3% to 3,630 pence.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0640 GMT - Burberry appears to have the right building blocks in place for the next stage of its turnaround strategy, RBC Capital Markets' Piral Dadhania and Richard Chamberlain say. For its fiscal first quarter, the British luxury group reported retail revenue of 433 million pounds. It posted a 1% decrease in comparable store sales, which was lower than the 3% decline anticipated by the consensus. "Burberry's quarterly revenue growth has sequentially improved for five quarters," the analysts say. A strategy refocus towards outerwear offers more authenticity in a less competitive product category, they add. Shares have jumped around 27% over the year to date. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

Contact us in London. +44-20-7842-9288
Markettalk.eu@dowjones.com

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0748 GMT - Valterra Platinum's third-quarter earnings should improve substantially as production recovers and it gets a boost from rising platinum group metals prices, RBC Capital Markets analysts write. This follows a warning from the miner Friday that it expects a fall in first-half headline earnings due to lower sales volumes and costs associated with its demerger from Anglo American. Average PGM prices are up 24% since the beginning of June and its production is recovering, the analysts write. Its London-listed shares rise 4.3% to 3,630 pence.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0731 GMT - European oil companies trade up in opening trade after getting a boost from rising oil prices. Brent crude prices are up 0.8% at $70.08 a barrel as seasonal travel demand provides some support to prices, head of equity research at Hargreaves Lansdown Derren Nathan writes. In London, BP trades up 1.5% and Shell rises 1.2%. France's TotalEnergies rises 0.9%; Italy's Eni, Spain's Repsol and Portugal's Galp rise around 1%. Consumption in July is edging slightly above last year's levels to 105.2 million barrels a day, Nathan writes. However, tariff uncertainty and the prospect of a glut of oil supply means markets haven't managed to fully erase losses seen earlier in the week, he adds.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0728 GMT - Saab full-year consensus earnings estimates could rise by 2%-4% after it delivered a strong second-quarter performance, SEB analysts write. Despite orders falling 28% on year in the quarter, they still landed 5% above consensus, the bank says. Adjusted EBIT was 11% above consensus, driven by strong sales. Saab also raised its 2025 organic growth guidance to 16%-20% from 12%-16%. SEB has a hold rating and SEK490 target price. Shares rise 11% to 531 kronor. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0706 GMT - Bitcoin rises on optimism that U.S. regulation will encourage wider cryptocurrency adoption. The House on Thursday passed the Genius Act bill that will create a new regulatory framework for stablecoins, a cryptocurrency pegged to another asset such as the dollar. The House also passed a broader crypto market structure bill known as the Clarity Act. Meanwhile, the Financial Times reported that Trump will sign an executive order that opens the retirement market up to alternative investments including cryptocurrencies. "The regulatory environment remains supportive and we see further upside in crypto assets," Jefferies economist Mohit Kumar says in a note. Bitcoin rises 0.8% to $120,444, LSEG data show. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0702 GMT - Swiss chemical company Clariant is under significant pressure as it is now involved in a sixth lawsuit regarding anti-competitive practices in the ethylene purchasing market, Vontobel analyst Sibylle Bischofberger writes. The additional lawsuit from U.S.-listed chemical company LyondellBasell is not good for sentiment and no final decision is expected soon, Bischofberger adds. Clariant shares closed Thursday at 8.54 Swiss francs.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0656 GMT - Nordic markets are seen opening slightly higher. IG calls the OMXS30 up 0.5% at around 2568. Better-than-expected U.S. macro data and good earnings reports helped lift the S&P 500 0.5% to a new all-time high Thursday, SEB chief economist Jens Magnusson writes. Europe also had a good day with gains almost everywhere, and the Eurostoxx 50 climbed 1.5%. Overnight, the positive stock market trend has continued in Asia with gains in most places, SEB says. An exception is the Tokyo Stock Exchange, where the Nikkei 225 is lower ahead of this weekend's parliamentary election. Stock market futures indicate that we may see positive openings in both Europe and the U.S. when today's trading starts, Magnusson says. OMXS30 closed at 2555.63, OMXN40 at 2318.00 and OBX at 1533.71. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0653 GMT - U.K. consumer-goods group Reckitt Benckiser should get a reputational boost from the sale of its Essential Home business to Advent International, RBC Capital Markets' James Edwardes Jones and Wassachon Fon Udomsilpa say in a research note. The credibility of Reckitt's executives was resting on delivering a deal and the move should enable investors to focus more closely on the group's core operations, the analysts say. Reckitt expects to return $2.2 billion to shareholders via a special dividend. "We think this is actually in excess of the immediate net proceeds that Reckitt can expect of $1.3 billion," RBC says. Shares closed at 49.82 pounds Thursday. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0652 GMT - Food Empire Holdings is likely expanding its brand awareness across Asia, CGS International analysts say in a research report. The Singapore-listed company and Malaysia's Capital A have unveiled a strategic partnership to co-develop and launch a new range of ready-to-drink beverages. Collaboration between their subsidiaries will kick off with a Vietnamese iced coffee product to be sold on AirAsia flights and via retail channels across Asia. Food Empire's efforts to grow its business and the MAS' upcoming S$5 billion Equity Market Development Program will probably re-rate the company's valuations, the analysts say. The brokerage raises the stock's target price to S$2.73 from S$2.28 with an unchanged add rating. Shares are 6.6% higher at S$2.26. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0651 GMT - Alimentation Couche-Tard's withdrawn offer for Seven & i caps a wild year for the Tokyo-listed stock. The stock is back near levels seen in August after the scrapped $47 billion takeover bid sent shares of 7-Eleven's owner down about 12.5% over two days. From hereon, Seven & i's share price will likely be supported by its continuing buyback, says Jefferies analyst Shunsuke Kuriyama. He sees Seven & i's continued commitment to its standalone path and returning Y2 trillion through buybacks by fiscal 2030 as reassuring. "The market will now focus more on the fundamental recovery of its operations in SEJ and SEI," he writes in a note, referring to the retail giant's Japanese and North American convenience-store units, respectively. (farah.elias@wsj.com)

0640 GMT - Burberry appears to have the right building blocks in place for the next stage of its turnaround strategy, RBC Capital Markets' Piral Dadhania and Richard Chamberlain say. For its fiscal first quarter, the British luxury group reported retail revenue of 433 million pounds. It posted a 1% decrease in comparable store sales, which was lower than the 3% decline anticipated by the consensus. "Burberry's quarterly revenue growth has sequentially improved for five quarters," the analysts say. A strategy refocus towards outerwear offers more authenticity in a less competitive product category, they add. Shares have jumped around 27% over the year to date. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

0636 GMT - TSMC is likely to raise wafer prices by around 3%-5% globally in USD terms given strong demand for advanced chips and unfavorable foreign-exchange rates, Morgan Stanley analysts say in a research note. They think TSMC's gross profit margin next year will be significantly above its guidance of "53% and higher" following the potential price hike. Nvidia's resumption of H20 chip shipments to China is good news for TSMC given the country's sizable AI market, the analysts add. AI graphics processing units in China are expected to contribute 2%-3% of TSMC's revenue in 2025. Meanwhile, TSMC's planned $165 billion investment in the U.S. could increase its chances of a tariff exemption, the analysts say. MS raises the stock's target price to NT$1,388.00 from NT$1,288.00 while maintaining an overweight rating. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0629 GMT - Burberry's brand turnaround plan seems to be on track, Citi analysts Thomas Chauvet and Mahesh Mohankumar write in a research note. The U.K. luxury-fashion firm posted retail revenue of 433 million pounds for its fiscal first quarter. Comparable store sales dropped 1% on year, beating consensus expectations of a 3% decline. This was the third consecutive quarter of improvement in like-for-like sales since CEO Joshua Schulman launched his growth plan last November, Citi says. This implies the company could return to growth in the second quarter for the first time in two years, the analysts say. "Burberry's strategic plan is robust," they say, Citi reiterates its buy rating on the stock. Shares closed at 12.48 pounds on Thursday. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

data.title: European shares rise as investors focus on earnings
data.description:
European shares rose on Friday, on course for small weekly gains, as investors focussed on corporate earnings for clues on the impact of U.S. tariff policies on businesses.

The pan-European STOXX 600 index rose 0.3% to 548.84 points, as of 0731 GMT, and was on track for a second weekly gain.

Other major regional indexes, including the German DAX , France's CAC 40 and Britain's FTSE 100 CURRENCYCOM:UK100 also rose modestly.

Wall Street indexes touched record highs on Thursday after strong economic data and earnings reports showed American consumers remained willing to spend.

Swedish defence material maker Saab jumped 12.7%, the top gainer in the STOXX 600, after it posted higher-than-expected second-quarter earnings and raised its sales outlook.

Vestas Wind Systems gained 8.8% after J.P. Morgan upgraded the Danish wind turbine maker to "overweight" from "neutral".

On the flip side, GSK fell more than 6% after a U.S. FDA advisory panel recommended against approving its blood cancer drug Blenrep.

Swedish home appliances maker Electrolux slumped 14.6% after poor second-quarter performance in Europe and India's Reliance Industries said its retail unit acquired home appliance maker Kelvinator from Electrolux.

data.title: India stocks, rupee, bonds swaps, call at 1:10 p.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was down 0.7% at 81,685, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 declined 0.7% to 24,944, due to broad-based losses led by financial stocks, as Axis Bank dropped following its quarterly profit miss.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee declined nearly 0.1% versus the U.S. dollar to 86.1550, as dollar bids from foreign and local private banks weighed on the rupee, with traders also pointing to heightened demand to buy dollars at the daily reference rate.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN063335G=CC) was quoted at 100.21 rupees, with the yield down slightly at 6.2965%, tracking a decline in U.S. Treasury yields, and as traders cheered strong demand in New Delhi's debt sale, especially for the longer-end.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was down over 1 basis point at 5.49%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) was down nearly 2 bps at 5.70%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 5.40%, and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 5.35%.

data.title: Asian shares head for best week in months on trade deal hopes; currencies fall
data.description:
Key points:
  • MSCI Asia index at over 3-year high
  • Thai stocks up 7% for the week, best since June 2020
  • Indonesia shares rise for 10th straight session
  • TSMC gains 2% on record quarterly profit

Stock markets in emerging Asia were headed for their best week in several months on Friday, driven by optimism around trade deals with the United States, and an increasingly dovish tilt at regional central banks.

The MSCI gauge of emerging Asia stocks (.MIMS00000PUS) touched its highest in more than three years and was set to end the week 1.6% higher. Stocks in Taiwan TWSE:TAIEX, which make up over a fifth of the index, rose 1% to a more than four-month high.

TSMC , the world's largest contract chipmaker, rallied more than 2% after posting a record quarterly profit on Thursday.

An MSCI index of ASEAN equities (.MISU00000PUS), dominated by Singapore, surged to a more than nine-month high. Singapore's stocks touched a record high for the tenth straight day, which also marks its longest ever rally.

Indonesian equities jumped more than 1% to an over seven-month peak, led by data centre service provider DCI Indonesia , which surged over 16% to a record high, and Barito Renewables Energy , which gained about 8%.

A trade deal with the U.S. at a lower than previously announced tariff rate, alongside an increasingly dovish stance by the central bank, has helped drive inflows into equities.

Domestic investors have net bought 6.21 trillion rupiah ($380.86 million) so far this month, as per exchange data.

In Thailand, the benchmark index rose 1%. It has surged almost 7% for the week and was set for its biggest gain since June 2020, driven by inflows on news of the government proposing a new central bank chief with a dovish bias.

Elsewhere, Malaysian stocks jumped 0.4%, while those in the Philippines ticked marginally higher. India's Nifty 50 slipped 0.7% to a more than three-week low.

Currencies in the region were mixed. Indonesia's rupiah , the Philippine peso , Thai baht , and Singapore's dollar advanced modestly against the U.S. dollar, while Malaysia's ringgit and the Indian rupee slipped.

Asian currencies have lost some ground over the past few sessions as the dollar index recovered from a more than two-year low hit earlier this month.

"Asian currencies have come under pressure amid an incipient rebound in the U.S. dollar, driven by renewed market focus on tariffs," MUFG senior currency analyst Lloyd Chan said.

"And while markets still hold net short positions on the U.S. dollar, some unwinding of those trades could lend support for the recent U.S. dollar rebound."

HIGHLIGHTS:

** Indonesia's 10-year benchmark yield (ID10YT=RR) slips to 6.558%

** Indonesia still negotiating tariff details

** Malaysia's economy likely grew 4.5% y/y in Q2

** Singapore's exports rise 13% annually in June

data.title: Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders shares fall 2% in Friday's trading session
data.description:
Shares of Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders declined by 2% to trade at Rs 2,987 in Friday's session. The stock is part of the NIFTY MIDCAP 150 index.

In corporate announcements, the Board of Directors approved the proposal for the acquisition of a controlling and substantial stake of Colombo Dockyard PLC through an investment not exceeding USD 52.96 million on June 27, 2025. Additionally, the trading window for dealing in securities of MDL by designated persons and their immediate relatives remains closed from July 1, 2025, until 48 hours after the declaration of financial results for the quarter ending June 30, 2025.

Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders announced an interim dividend of Rs 3 per share (60%) on April 8, 2025, with an effective date of April 16, 2025. Prior dividends include Rs 23.19 per share (231.9%)interim dividend on October 17, 2024, and Rs 12.11 per share (121.1%) final dividend on May 29, 2024.

The company had announced a stock split on October 22, 2024, with the old face value of Rs 10 being split into a new face value of Rs 5. The ex-split and record date for the same was December 27, 2024.

data.title: SNG: New Fortress Energy to deploy 2nd FSRU in Egypt
data.description:
Staff Energy

Arab Finance: New Fortress Energy Inc. is set to deploy the Energos Winter, a 138,250 cubic meters floating storage and regasification unit (FSRU), in Egypt as early as August 2025, according to a press release.

This falls within New Fortress Energy’s five-year agreement with the Egyptian Natural Gas Holding Company (EGAS) through its subsidiary, marking the group’s second FSRU in Egypt.

The Energos Winter will operate at EGAS’ liquefied natural gas (LNG) import terminal located at Damietta, joining the Energos Eskimo.

Chris Guinta, CFO of New Fortress Energy, commented: “This deal enhances NFE’s goals of providing reliable and cost-effective energy across the globe.”

On his part, Yasseen Mohamed, Executive Managing Director of EGAS, stated: “EGAS is pleased to strengthen its long-standing partnership with New Fortress Energy through the execution of a second Regasification Service Agreement.”

“Under this agreement, NFE’s second FSRU, Energos Winter, will provide regasification services at the Damietta terminal, contributing to the security of natural gas supply for Egypt over the next five years,” added Mohamed.

data.title: Eurozone Bond Yields Edge Higher — Market Talk
data.description: Eurozone government bond yields edge higher in early trade as solid corporate earnings lift sentiment and dent demand for safe-haven assets. Global inflation concerns linger, however. Ten-year eurozone bond yields rise about 2 basis points, according to Tradeweb. The 10-year Bund yield is last at 2.692%. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Nordnet shares slide after Q2 core profit miss
data.description:
** Shares in Nordnet fall around 5% after the Swedish online broker reported a second-quarter adjusted operating profit that slightly missed market expectations

** J.P.Morgan notes the result of SEK 893 million missed consensus by 1%, driven by a 6% shortfall in commission income that was only partly offset by stronger-than-expected net interest income

** The broker also points out that the profit miss was cushioned by operating expenses coming in 2% lower than consensus, showing good cost control

** Nordnet also guided for full-year net interest income of around SEK 2.3 billion, which is about 2% above current consensus forecasts, JPM adds

** The stock is heading for its biggest one-day drop since April if losses hold

data.title: Asker Healthcare to Buy Medical Equipment Provider Dartin
data.description:
Asker Healthcare Group agreed to acquire neonatal care medical equipment and elderly patient lifting devices distributor Dartin.

The Sweden-listed medical products company expects the acquisition to be completed in the third quarter of 2025, it said Friday.

The target company, based in the Czech Republic and Slovakia, logged 46 million kronor of revenue in 2024.

data.title: Shell Catalysts & Technologies, Technip Energies sign global alliance agreement for carbon capture delivery
data.description:
Shell Catalysts & Technologies and Technip Energies have signed their global alliance agreement to work exclusively together to deliver a post-combustion, amine-based carbon capture solution using Shell’s CANSOLV1 CO? Capture System, as per Hydrocarbonprocessing.

The alliance combines Shell Catalysts & Technologies leading technology expertise with Technip Energies’ integration and project delivery excellence. The parties have agreed to work exclusively together to jointly offer an enhanced, complementary solution in post-combustion carbon capture space, aiming to deliver maximum value to customers.

Together, Shell Catalysts & Technologies and Technip Energies believe that this is the best model to make carbon capture more investable, scalable and accessible for industrial sectors—helping customers to decarbonize. From project inception through to operational support, the alliance integrates expertise and delivers committed collaboration, work closely with clients to ensure seamless execution and continuous performance optimization.

Robin Mooldijk, President Projects & Technology, Shell said: “This agreement marks a significant milestone in a relationship built on shared ambition and delivery. Through our strengthened alliance with Technip Energies, we’re helping customers advance their decarbonization plans, backed by deep expertise and more than a decade of working side by side.”

Arnaud Pieton, Chief Executive Officer, Technip Energies said: “By forming a global alliance with Shell Catalysts & Technologies in the field of carbon capture, we combine cutting-edge technology, smart engineering and excellence in project execution. This global alliance is the result of more than 10 years of collaboration and continuous innovation. Our ambition is to deliver a world designed to last by enabling hard-to-abate industries to decarbonize with greater certainty and affordably. Canopy by T.EN™ powered by Shell CANSOLV® carbon capture solution pragmatically delivers on this ambition."

The Shell Catalysts & Technologies and Technip Energies alliance builds on a strong foundation of proven performance, with two operating CANSOLV facilities, and four CANSOLV-based projects reaching a final investment decision within the last 24 months. This includes Net Zero Teesside Power, a pioneering project set to be the world’s first gas-fired power station with carbon capture and storage.?During this time, both companies have continually refined their offer through innovation, standardization and operational insight to meet dynamic market needs.

data.title: UK's Senior hits 6-year high on $268 million Aerostructures business sale, share buyback
data.description:
** Shares of British engineering firm Senior up nearly 17% at 221p, at highest level since July 2019

** On track to record biggest intraday pct gain since May 2021

** Co to sell Aerostructures business for up to 200 mln pounds ($268.60 mln) to Sullivan Street Partners

** To buyback shares worth 40 mln pounds

** "Post the sale of Aerostructures, Senior will have a much improved EBITA margin and ROCE profile, stronger operating cash flow dynamics, lower capital intensity and will be well-positioned to grow/outperform its core end markets," says brokerage Jefferies

** Including session gains, stock up about 39% YTD

($1 = 0.7446 pounds)

data.title: UK's Ofcom Amends Annual License Fees for Three Spectrum Bands
data.description:
The UK's Office of Communications, or Ofcom, revised the annual license fees for three bands of spectrum used by mobile operators in the country.

The regulator said Friday the fees for 900-megahertz and 1800 MHz spectrums will fall by 26% from their current levels, while the fees for 2100 MHz spectrum will rise by 6%. The fee changes will take effect upon issuance of related regulations later in 2025.

Meanwhile, Ofcom launched a consultation process for a planned amendment of the Mobile Trading Regulations to remove the need for mobile network operators to pay all installments of the fees before trading the spectrum they hold. Interested parties may provide their feedback until Sept. 12.

data.title: Sunway Unit JV Secures Leasehold Land in Singapore for SG$704 Million
data.description:
Sunway unit Sunway Developments, jointly with Sing Holdings Residential secured a 99-year leasehold land parcel at Chuan Grove, Singapore, for SG$703.6 million, according to a Thursday Malaysian bourse filing.

Shares gained 2% recently on Friday.

The 15,831.5-square-metre plot was awarded by Singapore's Urban Redevelopment Authority.

A joint venture company will be formed, with Sing Holdings Residential owning a 65% stake and Sunway Developments holding the remaining 35%, to undertake the residential development.

The project is expected to begin contributing positively to the company's earnings, it said.

data.title: Tata Communications, NMDC, JK Cement among top gainers on Nifty Midcap 150
data.description:
At 1:00 pm on Friday, a few stocks emerged as top gainers on the Nifty Midcap 150. Tata Comm was trading at Rs 1,764.00, with a gain of 1.87%. NMDC stood at Rs 70.80, up by 1.78%. J. K. Cement was at Rs 6,542.00, with a 1.73% increase. Gujarat Fluoro was trading at Rs 3,495.20, gaining 1.49%, while Dalmia Bharat was at Rs 2,221.70, up by 1.47%.

Tata Comm has shown consistent financial performance over the years. The consolidated revenue for the quarter-ending June 2025 stood at Rs 5,959.85 Crore, compared to Rs 5,633.37 Crore in June 2024. The net profit for the same quarter was Rs 183.63 Crore, a decrease from Rs 329.90 Crore in the previous year.

The company’s annual consolidated revenue has also seen growth, with Rs 23,108.59 Crore reported for the year-ending March 2025, up from Rs 20,968.82 Crore in March 2024. However, the net profit decreased from Rs 1,766.84 Crore in March 2023 to Rs 949.59 Crore in March 2024, but increased to Rs 1,583.15 Crore in March 2025.

Key financial ratios for Tata Comm as of March 2025 include a basic EPS of Rs 64.44 and a diluted EPS of Rs 64.39. The book value per share was Rs 106.01. The company’s return on net worth was 60.78%, and it reported a debt-to-equity ratio of 3.60.

The company announced several corporate actions recently. On July 17, 2025, announcements were made regarding the raising of funds through non-convertible debentures and other restructuring activities. Additionally, amendments were made to the insider trading policy.

data.title: Japan futures hit highest since May 27 on weather woes, post 5th weekly gain
data.description:
• Japanese rubber futures closed Friday at a nearly seven-week high, rising for the fifth consecutive week, on concerns that heavy rains could disrupt tapping in key Southeast Asian producing regions.

  • The Osaka Exchange (OSE) rubber contract for December delivery , added 3.9 yen, or 1.2%, to 329.1 yen ($2.21) per kg, its highest close since May 27, and logged a 3.72% gain for the week.

  • The rubber contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) for September delivery rose 240 yuan, or 1.65%, to 14,810 yuan ($2,063.08) per metric ton.

  • The most active August butadiene rubber contract on the SHFE (SHBRv1) gained 200 yuan, or 1.74%, to 11,720 yuan ($1,632.63) per metric ton.

  • Top rubber producer Thailand's meteorological agency warned of heavy rain and accumulations that may cause flash floods and overflows from July 19-24.

  • Heavy rain from the ongoing tropical depression is expected to affect key rubber production areas in Southeast Asia over the next three days, which may disrupt rubber tapping operations, said broker Everbright Futures.

  • Meanwhile, China's rubber output rose 2% year-on-year in the first half of 2025, even as the country's tyre production fell 1.1% from a year earlier in June, data from the National Bureau of Statistics showed.

  • Major Japanese automaker Mazda's U.S. sales fell 18.6% in May and 6.5% in June following the implementation of 25% tariffs on automobiles.

  • The auto industry makes up about 28% of Japan's exports to the U.S.

  • Automobile sales could influence the intensity of vehicle manufacturing, which involves using rubber-made tyres.

  • The front-month rubber contract on the Singapore Exchange's SICOM platform for August delivery last traded at 170 U.S. cents per kg, up 1.1%.

($1 = 148.7200 yen)

($1 = 7.1786 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Vijaya Diagnostic Board to Consider Q1 FY26 Results on July 28, 2025
data.description:
Vijaya Diagnostic Centre Limited has announced that a meeting of its Board of Directors is scheduled for July 28, 2025, to consider and approve the standalone and consolidated unaudited financial results for the first quarter ended June 30, 2025. The trading window, which was closed on July 1, 2025, will remain closed until 48 hours after the announcement of the financial results, concluding on July 30, 2025.

Board Meeting DetailsParticulars

Details

Meeting Date

July 28, 2025Purpose

Consider and approve Q1 FY26 financial resultsFinancial Results

Standalone and Consolidated UnauditedTrading Window Closure

Closed from July 1, 2025, to July 30, 2025

Financial Performance ReviewThe Board will review the company's financial performance for the first quarter of FY26, examining key metrics in both standalone and consolidated terms. This assessment will provide insights into the company's growth trajectory and operational efficiency during the period.Operational HighlightsIn addition to financial figures, the board will likely discuss operational highlights from the first quarter. These may include the volume of diagnostic tests conducted, expansion of services, technological upgrades, and other strategic initiatives that have influenced the company's performance.Corporate MattersThe board meeting will also address other corporate matters, which may include discussions on strategic initiatives, business development, and compliance-related issues. These discussions are crucial for guiding the company's future direction and ensuring sustainable growth.Market ImpactThe announcement of the board meeting and the subsequent release of the financial results are important events for investors and market participants. The market will be closely watching the company's performance to gauge its growth potential and overall financial health.

data.title: Copper rises on upbeat US data, China demand prospects
data.description:
Copper prices rose on Friday, supported by stronger-than-expected U.S. economic data and expectations of increased buying from top consumer China after the recent price dip.

Three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange gained 0.36% to $9,701.5 per metric ton by 0700 GMT, rising 0.49% so far this week.

The most-traded copper contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange added 0.65% to 78,410 yuan ($10,922.74) a ton, but was still 0.28% lower this week.

"The U.S. economic data has been encouraging, boosting the hope for better copper demand and lowering the chances for immediate interest rate cuts," a Beijing-based metals analyst at a futures company said.

U.S. retail sales increased 0.6% in June after an unrevised 0.9% drop in May, while the number of Americans filing new applications for unemployment benefits fell last week, pointing to steady job growth so far in July.

"LME copper stocks have been rising, mainly at its Asia warehouses as some traders may be betting on more buying by China with recent price drops, uncertain whether this will materialise and by how much," a Shanghai-based metals analyst at a futures company said.

Total copper stocks at the LME-registered warehouses (MCUSTX-TOTAL) rose 34.8% since the end of June to 122,150 tons as of Thursday, with the spikes mainly at Asian warehouses such as in Gwangyang and Taiwan.

The focus is also on looming U.S. tariffs as the August 1 deadline nears and on details of the 50% import levies on copper.

LME tin gained 0.68% to $33,240 a ton, zinc advanced 0.42% to $2,748.5, lead (CNPB3) added 0.2% to $1,977, aluminium rose 0.19% at $2,583, and nickel up 0.09% to $15,110.

SHFE tin gained the most by 1.07% to 264,540 yuan a ton, zinc jumped 1% to 22,300 yuan, nickel rose 0.73% to 120,500 yuan, and aluminium grew 0.42% to 20,510 yuan. Lead fell 0.3% to 16,820 yuan.

For the top stories in metals and other news, click

or

($1 = 7.1786 Chinese yuan)
